endpoints.name: Galarian Meowth (Rebel Clash 126/192, Steel-type regional variant)

description: Two card-endpoints, both depict National Pokedex no. 52 Meowth, two designer-coordinated regional adaptations that diverge into separate evolution lines (Kanto Meowth -> Persian via level 28; Galarian Meowth -> Perrserker via the Galar-exclusive Steel-type line). The Kanto print is the cream civilization-adjacent civilian (golden coin, urban rooftop, *wanders the city in search of loose change*); the Galarian print is the disheveled iron-toughened Viking-cat (iron coin, Norwegian-Forest-Cat designer origin, *living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokemon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron*). Mirror sighed bidirectionally. Mirrors the wave 16 E2 Kantonian-Galarian Farfetch'd pair precedent shape-for-shape. Galarian Meowth already attaches to the galarian-regional-forms cohort node (no further wiring there); this 1:1 anchors the species-level Kanto/Galarian split directly so the Kanto print isn't an orphan in the regional-variant story.
citations: [PokemonTCG.io: sm3-101 (Kanto Meowth, Kagemaru Himeno art) + swsh2-126 (Galarian Meowth, Misa Tsutsui art)]
[In-corpus galarian-meowth.md trivia pass canonicalizing Norwegian-Forest-Cat / Viking longship design origin per pokejungle.net Origin of Species]
other_candidates_considered: castform-forms-cycle Mr. Nodeley at 2/4 — viable but one-Nodeley-per-invocation rule (already spent on alola-elite-four E3); defer to next pass when 3/4 threshold may also land
spiritomb / sigilyph / golett ancient-bound-entities speculative cluster — too thin, no designer-confirmed coordination
5ban Graphics / Toyste Beach artist nodes — threshold genuinely unclear per prompt; defer
kanto-meowth-line node (Meowth + Persian only — three regional Meowth forms split into separate evolution lines mean a node-per-line is structurally cleaner than a unified Meowth-species node)
